mova.smallDescription: An app for personal trainers to move from scattered tools to one structured system for managing clients and workflows.
fullDescription: MOVA is a multi-tenant SaaS built end-to-end as a solo founder project. It replaces fragmented workflows based on spreadsheets, WhatsApp and manual PDFs, consolidating client management, training, nutrition and progress tracking into a single mobile system, built around real-world use cases and highly customizable workflows.
Designed through direct collaboration with personal trainers, translating real workflows into scalable product decisions.
mova.challenge: Personal trainers lose 3–4 hours daily managing clients through spreadsheets, messaging apps and disconnected PDFs. There was no mobile-first, all-in-one tool built specifically around their real workflow — most solutions are either too generic or too expensive.
mova.solution: Built MOVA as a multi-tenant SaaS from scratch — a React Native app backed by a Supabase architecture with per-workspace data isolation (RLS), real-time sync, and role-based access. Designed 40+ screens in Figma before writing a single line of code, ensuring the UX actually fits how trainers think.

features.title: Client Management
features.description: Full client profiles with history, goals, physical data and progress over time. Trainers manage 10+ clients without switching tools.

features.title: Training Plan Builder
features.description: Create structured training plans with exercises, sets, reps and rest. Assign to any client in seconds. Estimated 70% reduction in plan creation time.

features.title: Nutrition Tracking
features.description: Design meal plans with macro targets per client. Supports multiple daily schedules and dietary restrictions.

features.title: Multi-tenant Architecture
features.description: Each trainer gets an isolated workspace. Row-Level Security on PostgreSQL ensures zero data leakage between accounts.

elephai.smallDescription: Rescued and rebuilt a failing Flutter app — from unstable pre-launch codebase to production-ready in under 8 weeks.
fullDescription: ElephAi is a B2C/B2B mobile app by Unwind Minds. I joined as Lead Developer when the app was stuck in a critical pre-launch state — unstable, tightly coupled, and blocked from going live. I took full ownership of the technical recovery: refactored the entire codebase, established a clean architecture, and delivered the first successful public release to both App Store and Google Play.
elephai.challenge: The app had been in development for months but was nowhere near production-ready. The codebase had no architectural boundaries, deeply coupled modules, and no clear ownership. The team needed someone to take the wheel, assess the damage, and ship.
elephai.solution: Took full technical ownership from day one. Audited the entire codebase, identified critical failure points, and rebuilt core modules with clear separation of concerns. Established architectural standards, documented decisions, and coordinated the App Store / Google Play release pipeline — delivering a stable, public-ready app in under 8 weeks.

features.title: Full Codebase Refactor
features.description: 100% of core modules rebuilt. Eliminated all tightly coupled dependencies and introduced clean architectural boundaries that scale with the team.

features.title: Architecture Leadership
features.description: Defined and documented all technical decisions, folder structure, state management patterns and API integration standards for the project.

features.title: App Store & Play Store Release
features.description: Configured signing, build pipelines and metadata for both stores. Delivered the app's first successful public launch from zero infrastructure.

features.title: Ongoing Feature Development
features.description: Continuing to build new product features and integrations (including AI API) while maintaining architectural quality and velocity.

nearsoft-onboarding.smallDescription: Configurable digital onboarding platform deployed across multiple financial institutions — replacing manual workflows with a fully automated, CMS-driven process.
fullDescription: Built at Nearsoft, a fintech software company based in Madeira, this platform digitizes the entire client onboarding journey for banks and financial institutions. The system is multi-bank, white-label ready, and CMS-driven — meaning non-technical teams can configure onboarding flows, update content, and manage approval steps without touching a single line of code. I owned the frontend architecture and the Directus CMS integration end-to-end.
nearsoft-onboarding.challenge: Financial institutions were running onboarding through paper forms, manual data entry, and fragmented internal tools — creating bottlenecks, compliance risks, and poor customer experience. The platform needed to be robust enough for regulated industries, flexible enough to serve multiple banks, and fast enough for customers to complete on mobile in minutes.
nearsoft-onboarding.solution: Architected a configurable frontend using React, Next.js and TypeScript with a Directus CMS backend (REST + GraphQL). Each onboarding step is dynamically driven by CMS configuration, enabling different institutions to have fully customized flows without code changes. Integrated browser APIs for document capture, step persistence and real-time validation. Set up CI/CD pipelines and linting to harden code quality and speed up team onboarding.

features.title: Multi-bank CMS Architecture
features.description: A single Directus CMS instance powers independent onboarding flows for multiple financial institutions. Non-technical teams control step logic, content and validation rules — no deployments needed. Reduced support tickets by 80%.

features.title: KYC & Document Capture
features.description: Integrated browser APIs for in-browser document scanning and identity verification, enabling fully remote KYC compliance without redirecting users to third-party tools.

features.title: Step Persistence & Recovery
features.description: Users can abandon and resume the onboarding flow at any point. Browser-native APIs handle state persistence, preventing data loss and reducing drop-off rates.

features.title: CI/CD & Code Quality
features.description: Introduced ESLint, Prettier and GitHub Actions CI pipelines from scratch. Standardized code style across the team and automated quality gates, cutting integration bugs and accelerating developer onboarding.

features.title: Internal AI Chatbot Integration
features.description: Contributed frontend UI and API integration for an internal AI chatbot prototype powered by a locally hosted LLM (Ollama), as part of a parallel innovation initiative within the company.

features.title: Responsive & Accessible Interfaces
features.description: Pixel-perfect implementation of design system components across all devices. Followed accessibility standards to ensure compliance with financial sector requirements.

nearsoft-llm-chatbot.smallDescription: On-premise AI chatbot built with Ollama and Llama 3 — connecting private APIs and CMS data sources to automate internal information retrieval with zero cloud exposure.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.fullDescription: Most companies send their internal data to third-party AI APIs — exposing sensitive information to the cloud. This prototype, built during my time at Nearsoft, took the opposite approach: a fully local LLM stack using Ollama and Llama 3, running entirely on company infrastructure. I designed and built the complete system — from the Express.js API layer connecting to private CMS and internal data sources, to the React chat interface where teams could query company knowledge in real time. It wasn't just a demo. It proved that enterprise-grade AI tooling is possible without a single byte leaving the building.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.challenge: Internal teams were losing significant time manually searching across disconnected tools — private APIs, CMS platforms, internal docs — to find information that should have been instantly accessible. Off-the-shelf AI solutions were either too expensive, too generic, or required sending proprietary data to external cloud providers, which was a non-starter in a regulated industry context.
nearsoft-llm-chatbot.solution: Designed and built a full-stack AI chatbot running 100% on-premise using Ollama to serve Llama 3 locally. Built an Express.js backend that aggregated data from private APIs and CMS platforms into a unified context layer fed to the LLM. Built the frontend conversation interface with React, handling real-time streaming responses, conversation state, and low-latency UX. The result: a working internal knowledge assistant that demonstrated local LLMs are viable for enterprise workflows — with no cloud dependency, no data exposure, and no per-query cost.

features.title: Fully On-Premise LLM Stack
features.description: Ollama serves Llama 3 locally on company hardware — no API keys, no cloud costs, no data leaving the building. Validated that production-quality LLM inference is achievable without external dependencies.

features.title: Private API & CMS Aggregation
features.description: Express.js backend connects to internal APIs and CMS platforms, aggregating fragmented data sources into a unified context layer. The chatbot answers questions that previously required manual lookup across multiple tools.

features.title: Real-Time Streaming Chat Interface
features.description: React frontend with streaming response support, conversation history management, and optimistic UI patterns — ensuring a fast, fluid experience even when querying large internal datasets.

features.title: Low-Latency Response Architecture
features.description: API integration logic was designed for reliability and speed — minimizing round-trip time between the frontend, the Express layer, and the local LLM. Validated response quality and latency under realistic usage conditions.

features.title: Enterprise AI Feasibility Proof
features.description: The prototype directly demonstrated that local LLMs can replace cloud AI in internal tooling contexts — laying the technical and conceptual groundwork for future AI-enabled workflows within the company.

features.title: Multi-Source Context Injection
features.description: Engineered a context-building pipeline that pulls relevant data from multiple internal sources and injects it into the LLM prompt — enabling accurate, context-aware answers grounded in real company data.
